what: Search for the required sample size
title: Getting started with anovapowersim
text: easiest way get required sample size use power_n() search sample size needed reach requested power. example 2 x 2 mixed design one -subjects factor (cond) one within-subject factor (stim). specify interested cond:stim interaction, want 90% power detect partial eta squared 0.14. power_n() search required sample size per -subject cell, n = 17 gives total N = 34. Note: use 1000 simulations quick example, package defaults 10000 simulations precise estimates. output table uses compact column names: n_per_cell sample size per -subject cell, total_n full sample size, num_df den_df ANOVA degrees freedom, ncp noncentrality parameter, power_calc noncentral F power calculation, power_sim simulation estimate.
code: power_n(   between = c(cond = 2), # cond has 2 levels   within = c(stim = 4), # stim has 4 levels   term = "cond:stim",   target_pes = 0.14,   alpha = 0.05,   power = 0.90,   n_sims = 1000, # use 5000+ for a more precise estimate   seed = 123 # for reproducibility ) #> <anovapowersim_curve> #>   term:          'cond:stim' #>   target power:  0.900 #>   alpha:         0.05 #>   effect size:   pes = 0.14 #>   n values:      6 per-cell sample sizes visited #>   sims per cell size: 1000 #>   SS type:       III #>   n needed for between-subjects cell: 17 #>   total N needed: 34 #>  #>  n_per_cell total_n n_sims num_df den_df    ncp power_calc power_sim #>          13      26   1000      3     72 11.721      0.808     0.795 #>          16      32   1000      3     90 14.651      0.897     0.885 #>          17      34   1000      3     96 15.628      0.917     0.912 #>          18      36   1000      3    102 16.605      0.934     0.936 #>          20      40   1000      3    114 18.558      0.958     0.946 #>          26      52   1000      3    150 24.419      0.991     0.991

previous_headings: Search for the required sample size
title: Getting started with anovapowersim
text: can add factors levels needed, specify term interest. , example want add condition 3 levels, interested 3-way interaction, can :
code: power_n(   between = c(cond = 2, age = 3), # cond has 2 levels, age has 3 levels   within = c(stim = 4), # stim has 4 levels   term = "cond:stim:age",   target_pes = 0.14,   alpha = 0.05,   power = 0.90,   n_sims = 1000, # use 5000+ for a more precise estimate   seed = 123 # for reproducibility )

title: Getting started with anovapowersim
text: might want see power changes across range sample sizes. power_curve() simulates power across range sample sizes, can specify n_range. result tidy data frame can plot plot_power_curve().
code: pc <- power_curve(   between = c(cond = 2),   within = c(stim = 2),   term = "cond:stim",   target_pes = 0.14,   n_range = c(16, 20, 23, 28), # n per between-subject cell   n_sims = 1000,   seed = 123 ) pc #> <anovapowersim_curve> #>   term:          'cond:stim' #>   target power:  <not specified> #>   alpha:         0.05 #>   effect size:   pes = 0.14 #>   n values:      4 per-cell sample sizes visited #>   sims per cell size: 1000 #>   SS type:       III #>   n needed for between-subjects cell: <not reached> #>   total N needed: <not reached> #>  #>  n_per_cell total_n n_sims num_df den_df   ncp power_calc power_sim #>          16      32   1000      1     30 4.884      0.571     0.557 #>          20      40   1000      1     38 6.186      0.679     0.666 #>          23      46   1000      1     44 7.163      0.745     0.735 #>          28      56   1000      1     54 8.791      0.829     0.831 plot_power_curve(   pc,   power_lines = c(.80, .90) # adds horizontal lines at 80% and 90% power )

title: Getting started with anovapowersim
text: larger simulation runs, set parallel = TRUE. set cores, anovapowersim uses one fewer number available cores prints message chosen count. Set cores explicitly want fixed number cores.
code: power_curve(   between = c(cond = 2),   within = c(stim = 2),   term = "cond:stim",   target_pes = 0.14,   n_range = c(16, 20, 23, 28),   n_sims = 5000,   parallel = TRUE,   cores = 4,   seed = 123 )

title: Getting started with anovapowersim
text: default, anovapowersim calibrates simulated cell means empirical reference dataset requested partial eta squared fitted stats::aov() model. corresponds fitted ANOVA denominator-df noncentrality convention. Set gpower = TRUE want G*Power-style convention (using ’Cohen (1988) option within-subjects designs) lambda = total_n * f^2.
code: power_n(   between = c(cond = 2),   within = c(stim = 4),   term = "cond:stim",   target_pes = 0.14,   alpha = 0.05,   power = 0.90,   n_sims = 1000,   seed = 123,   gpower = TRUE ) #> <anovapowersim_curve> #>   term:          'cond:stim' #>   target power:  0.900 #>   alpha:         0.05 #>   effect size:   pes = 0.14 #>   n values:      6 per-cell sample sizes visited #>   sims per cell size: 1000 #>   G*Power convention: TRUE #>   SS type:       III #>   n needed for between-subjects cell: 45 #>   total N needed: 90 #>  #>  n_per_cell total_n n_sims num_df den_df    ncp power_calc power_sim #>          36      72   1000      3    210 11.721      0.823     0.829 #>          44      88   1000      3    258 14.326      0.899     0.889 #>          45      90   1000      3    264 14.651      0.906     0.907 #>          47      94   1000      3    276 15.302      0.919     0.919 #>          52     104   1000      3    306 16.930      0.944     0.936 #>          72     144   1000      3    426 23.442      0.989     0.990

what: anovapowersim — Simple power simulations for ANOVAs
title: anovapowersim — Simple power simulations for ANOVAs
text: anovapowersim designed make determining priori power ANOVAs easy possible. can add many within/factors many levels like. ’s need estimate condition means, SDs, repeated-measures correlations; just enter target partial eta squared. package simulates data estimates power based specified design. also provides direct power calculations comparison. Getting priori power 2 × 2 × 3 mixed interaction effect simple running following:
code: install.packages("anovapowersim") # if not already installed  library(anovapowersim)  power_n(   between = c(group = 2), # group has 2 levels   within = c(stim = 2, cond = 3), # stim has 2 levels, cond has 3   term = "group:stim:cond", # three-way interaction term   target_pes = 0.08, # target effect size   n_sims = 5000, # increase to 10000+ for more precise estimates   power = .90,   alpha = .05,   parallel = TRUE, # simulations will be run in parallel for speed   seed = 123 # for reproducibility ) #><anovapowersim_curve> #>  term:          'group:stim:cond' #>  target power:  0.900 #>  alpha:         0.05 #>  effect size:   pes = 0.08 #>  n values:      8 per-cell sample sizes visited #>  sims per cell size: 5000 #>  SS type:       III #>  n needed for between-subjects cell: 38 #>  total N needed: 76 #> #> n_per_cell total_n n_sims num_df den_df    ncp power_calc power_sim #>         31      62   5000      2    120 10.435      0.823     0.825 #>         37      74   5000      2    144 12.522      0.890     0.885 #>         38      76   5000      2    148 12.870      0.899     0.903 #>         39      78   5000      2    152 13.217      0.907     0.901 #>         40      80   5000      2    156 13.565      0.915     0.918 #>         41      82   5000      2    160 13.913      0.922     0.916 #>         46      92   5000      2    180 15.652      0.949     0.947 #>         62     124   5000      2    244 21.217      0.989     0.988

title: anovapowersim — Simple power simulations for ANOVAs

title: anovapowersim — Simple power simulations for ANOVAs
text: Azaad, S. (2026). priori power analysis ANOVA interaction effects anovapowersim R package: short introduction. https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/86rsy_v1.

title: anovapowersim — Simple power simulations for ANOVAs
text: anovapowersim designed simple easy use first, means limitations now. support: Covariates (ANCOVAs) Means-based simulations unbalanced designs Nonsphericity corrections (though might change) Specific interaction shapes (based means) Simple main effects/pairwise comparisons

title: anovapowersim — Simple power simulations for ANOVAs
text: recommend checking Superpower, handles limitations .

what: anovapowersim: Design-Based Power Simulation for Factorial ANOVA — anovapowersim-package
title: anovapowersim: Design-Based Power Simulation for Factorial ANOVA — anovapowersim-package
text: Simulation-based power analysis balanced factorial ANOVA designs. Given - within-subject factor structures, target partial eta squared, sample sizes, anovapowersim generates default term-specific cell means, simulates datasets sphericity, refits ANOVA stats, returns tidy power estimates ggplot2 power curve.

title: anovapowersim: Design-Based Power Simulation for Factorial ANOVA — anovapowersim-package
text: primary entry point power_curve(). accepts design specification, term name, target partial eta squared, sample sizes. returns object class anovapowersim_curve can printed, summarised, plotted plot_power_curve(). lower-level building blocks also exported users can compose custom simulations: balanced_anova_design() design_term_means() simulate_design_dataset() compute_scale_factor()

title: anovapowersim: Design-Based Power Simulation for Factorial ANOVA — anovapowersim-package
text: Maintainer: Shaheed Azaad sazaad@uni-muenster.de

what: Create a balanced factorial ANOVA design specification — balanced_anova_design
title: Create a balanced factorial ANOVA design specification — balanced_anova_design
text: Builds design object used power_curve(), design_term_means(), simulate_design_dataset(). object stores factor names, level counts, generated factor levels, /within cell grids.

title: Create a balanced factorial ANOVA design specification — balanced_anova_design
code: balanced_anova_design(between = NULL, within = NULL)

title: Create a balanced factorial ANOVA design specification — balanced_anova_design
text: Named integer vector -subject factor level counts, e.g. c(group = 2). Use NULL -subject factors. within Named integer vector within-subject factor level counts, e.g. c(time = 3). Use NULL within-subject factors.

title: Create a balanced factorial ANOVA design specification — balanced_anova_design
text: object class anovapowersim_design_spec.

title: Create a balanced factorial ANOVA design specification — balanced_anova_design
code: d <- balanced_anova_design(between = c(group = 2), within = c(time = 3)) d$between_cells #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   group  #>   <fct>  #> 1 group1 #> 2 group2 d$within_cells #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>   time  #>   <fct> #> 1 time1 #> 2 time2 #> 3 time3

what: Compute the mean-deviation scaling factor from a change in partial eta squared — compute_scale_factor
title: Compute the mean-deviation scaling factor from a change in partial eta squared — compute_scale_factor
text: Given existing partial eta squared term target partial eta squared, returns multiplier k must applied term's additive contribution cell means order obtain target effect size residual structure.

title: Compute the mean-deviation scaling factor from a change in partial eta squared — compute_scale_factor
code: compute_scale_factor(old_pes, new_pes)

title: Compute the mean-deviation scaling factor from a change in partial eta squared — compute_scale_factor
text: old_pes Numeric scalar (0, 1), numeric-looking character scalar ".310". current partial eta squared term interest. new_pes Numeric scalar (0, 1), numeric-looking character scalar ".200". target partial eta squared.

title: Compute the mean-deviation scaling factor from a change in partial eta squared — compute_scale_factor
text: single positive numeric value k. k > 1 amplifies effect, k < 1 shrinks , k == 1 leaves unchanged.

title: Compute the mean-deviation scaling factor from a change in partial eta squared — compute_scale_factor
text: derivation straightforward: partial eta squared can written pes = SS_effect / (SS_effect + C), C part denominator held fixed package's rescaling. Thus pes / (1 - pes) scales target effect's sum squares. Scaling term's deviations k scales target effect's sum squares k^2, required multiplier $$k = \sqrt{\frac{p_{\mathrm{new}} / (1 - p_{\mathrm{new}})}                       {p_{\mathrm{old}} / (1 - p_{\mathrm{old}})}}.$$

title: Compute the mean-deviation scaling factor from a change in partial eta squared — compute_scale_factor
code: compute_scale_factor(0.10, 0.05)   # shrink #> [1] 0.6882472 compute_scale_factor(0.05, 0.10)   # amplify #> [1] 1.452966

what: Build calibrated default means for a design term — design_term_means
title: Build calibrated default means for a design term — design_term_means
text: Creates default contrast pattern one ANOVA term scales exact reference dataset requested partial eta squared supplied balanced design assumptions.

title: Build calibrated default means for a design term — design_term_means
code: design_term_means(   design,   term,   target_pes,   n,   sd = 1,   r = 0.5,   gpower = FALSE,   ss_type = "III" )

title: Build calibrated default means for a design term — design_term_means
text: design anovapowersim_design_spec balanced_anova_design(). term Character scalar naming ANOVA term target. Interaction terms order-insensitive. target_pes Target partial eta squared. n Sample size per -subject cell. pure within designs, total sample size. sd Common outcome standard deviation. r Compound-symmetric correlation among within-subject cells. gpower Logical; TRUE, calibrate G*Power-style noncentrality convention lambda = total_n * f^2 (using 'Cohen (1988) option within-subjects designs). ss_type Sums--squares type tested ANOVA term. "III" default order-invariant tests unbalanced designs. Use "" reproduce sequential stats::aov() tests.

title: Build calibrated default means for a design term — design_term_means
text: numeric matrix cell means, rows indexing cells columns indexing within cells.

title: Build calibrated default means for a design term — design_term_means
code: d <- balanced_anova_design(between = c(group = 2), within = c(time = 2)) design_term_means(d, term = "group:time", target_pes = 0.2, n = 20) #>            [,1]       [,2] #> [1,]  0.2436699 -0.2436699 #> [2,] -0.2436699  0.2436699

what: Plot a simulation-based power curve — plot_power_curve
title: Plot a simulation-based power curve — plot_power_curve
text: Renders anovapowersim_curve ggplot2 line + ribbon horizontal reference requested power values , auto-search used, vertical marker estimated required total sample size.

title: Plot a simulation-based power curve — plot_power_curve
code: plot_power_curve(   x,   show_target = TRUE,   power_lines = NULL,   show_n_needed = TRUE,   ... )

title: Plot a simulation-based power curve — plot_power_curve
text: x anovapowersim_curve object power_curve(). show_target Logical; draw horizontal target power line (default TRUE). power_lines Optional numeric vector additional power reference lines, e.g. c(.80, .90). show_n_needed Logical; draw vertical line n_needed (default TRUE). ... Unused, S3 consistency.

title: Plot a simulation-based power curve — plot_power_curve
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot a simulation-based power curve — plot_power_curve
code: # \donttest{ pc <- power_curve(   between = c(group = 2),   within = c(time = 2),   term = "group:time",   target_pes = 0.2,   n_range = c(20, 30),   n_sims = 1000,   seed = 123 ) plot_power_curve(pc)  # }

what: Simulate ANOVA power from a balanced factorial design — power_curve
title: Simulate ANOVA power from a balanced factorial design — power_curve
text: Simulation-based power estimation balanced factorial designs sphericity. Users specify - within-subject factors, ANOVA term test, target partial eta squared, explicit sample sizes. function creates default contrast pattern target term, scales requested partial eta squared, simulates datasets, refits stats::aov(), estimates power counting p < alpha.

title: Simulate ANOVA power from a balanced factorial design — power_curve
code: power_curve(   between = NULL,   within = NULL,   term,   target_pes,   n_range,   n_sims = 10000,   alpha = 0.05,   ss_type = "III",   gpower = FALSE,   progress = interactive(),   parallel = FALSE,   cores = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate ANOVA power from a balanced factorial design — power_curve
text: Named integer vector -subject factor level counts, e.g. c(group = 2). Use NULL -subject factors. within Named integer vector within-subject factor level counts, e.g. c(time = 3, condition = 4). Use NULL within-subject factors. term Character scalar naming ANOVA term test, e.g. "group:time". Interaction terms order-insensitive; "time:group" resolves "group:time" design's factor order. target_pes Target partial eta squared term. n_range Integer vector sample sizes per -subject cell. pure within-subject designs, total sample size. n_sims Number simulated datasets per sample size. alpha Significance threshold. ss_type Sums--squares type tested ANOVA term. "III" default order-invariant tests unbalanced designs. Use "" reproduce sequential stats::aov() tests. gpower Logical; TRUE, calibrate means G*Power-style noncentrality convention lambda = total_n * f^2. default FALSE calibrates empirical reference dataset target_pes, equivalent lambda = den_df * f^2 fitted ANOVA. progress Logical; TRUE, show text progress bar. parallel Logical; TRUE, run simulations sample size via future ecosystem. cores Optional positive integer number cores use parallel = TRUE. NULL, uses one fewer number available cores, minimum one. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility.

title: Simulate ANOVA power from a balanced factorial design — power_curve
text: anovapowersim_curve object. $results tibble contains n_per_cell, total_n, n_sims, numerator denominator degrees freedom (num_df, den_df), noncentrality parameter (ncp), calculated power (power_calc), simulated power (power_sim).

title: Simulate ANOVA power from a balanced factorial design — power_curve
code: power_curve(   between = c(cond = 2),   within = c(stim = 2),   term = "cond:stim",   target_pes = 0.14,   n_range = c(16, 20, 23, 28), # n per between-subject cell   n_sims = 1000,   seed = 123 )  power_curve(   between = c(group = 2),   within = c(time = 2),   term = "group:time",   target_pes = 0.14,   n_range = c(12, 16, 20),   n_sims = 5000,   parallel = TRUE,   cores = 4,   seed = 123 )

what: Search for the sample size needed for target ANOVA power — power_n
title: Search for the sample size needed for target ANOVA power — power_n
text: Adaptive simulation search per--cell sample size needed reach requested power balanced factorial ANOVA design. search doubles upward n_start brackets target reaches n_max, refines bracket using interpolation midpoint bisection fallback.

title: Search for the sample size needed for target ANOVA power — power_n
code: power_n(   between = NULL,   within = NULL,   term,   target_pes,   power = 0.9,   n_sims = 10000,   alpha = 0.05,   ss_type = "III",   n_start = NULL,   n_max = 1000,   tol = 0.03,   gpower = FALSE,   progress = interactive(),   parallel = FALSE,   cores = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Search for the sample size needed for target ANOVA power — power_n
text: Named integer vector -subject factor level counts, e.g. c(group = 2). Use NULL -subject factors. within Named integer vector within-subject factor level counts, e.g. c(time = 3, condition = 4). Use NULL within-subject factors. term Character scalar naming ANOVA term test, e.g. "group:time". Interaction terms order-insensitive; "time:group" resolves "group:time" design's factor order. target_pes Target partial eta squared term. power Desired target power. n_sims Number simulated datasets per sample size. alpha Significance threshold. ss_type Sums--squares type tested ANOVA term. "III" default order-invariant tests unbalanced designs. Use "" reproduce sequential stats::aov() tests. n_start Starting sample size per -subject cell. NULL, starts smallest value can support empirical calibration requested design. n_max Maximum sample size per -subject cell. tol Acceptable precision target power. simulated value power also power + tol, power_n() warns requested precision band reached. gpower Logical; TRUE, calibrate means G*Power-style noncentrality convention lambda = total_n * f^2. default FALSE calibrates empirical reference dataset target_pes, equivalent lambda = den_df * f^2 fitted ANOVA. progress Logical; TRUE, show text progress bar. parallel Logical; TRUE, run simulations sample size via future ecosystem. cores Optional positive integer number cores use parallel = TRUE. NULL, uses one fewer number available cores, minimum one. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility.

title: Search for the sample size needed for target ANOVA power — power_n
text: anovapowersim_curve object n_needed total_n_needed. power_n(), n_needed always explicitly simulated n_per_cell value, never interpolated sample size. search reaches target power simulated value lands inside [power, power + tol], power_n() reports smallest explicitly simulated value target power warns requested precision band reached.

title: Search for the sample size needed for target ANOVA power — power_n
code: power_n(   between = c(cond = 2),   within = c(stim = 4),   term = "cond:stim",   target_pes = 0.14,   alpha = 0.05,   power = 0.90,   n_sims = 1000, # use 5000+ for a more precise estimate   seed = 123 # for reproducibility )

what: Print an anovapowersim power curve — print.anovapowersim_curve
title: Print an anovapowersim power curve — print.anovapowersim_curve

title: Print an anovapowersim power curve — print.anovapowersim_curve
code: # S3 method for class 'anovapowersim_curve' print(x, ...)

title: Print an anovapowersim power curve — print.anovapowersim_curve
text: x anovapowersim_curve object. ... Unused.

title: Print an anovapowersim power curve — print.anovapowersim_curve
text: Invisibly returns x.

what: Simulate data from a balanced ANOVA design — simulate_design_dataset
title: Simulate data from a balanced ANOVA design — simulate_design_dataset
text: Generates one long-format dataset balanced design. Supply means design_term_means() conformable matrix one row per -subject cell one column per within-subject cell.

title: Simulate data from a balanced ANOVA design — simulate_design_dataset
code: simulate_design_dataset(design, n, means, sd = 1, r = 0.5, empirical = FALSE)

title: Simulate data from a balanced ANOVA design — simulate_design_dataset
text: design anovapowersim_design_spec balanced_anova_design(). n Sample size per -subject cell. pure within designs, total sample size. means Numeric matrix population cell means. sd Common outcome standard deviation. r Compound-symmetric correlation among within-subject cells. empirical Logical; TRUE, use MASS::mvrnorm(empirical = TRUE) generated sample closely matches requested means/covariance.

title: Simulate data from a balanced ANOVA design — simulate_design_dataset
text: tibble ready stats::aov() columns id, factor columns, value.

title: Simulate data from a balanced ANOVA design — simulate_design_dataset
code: d <- balanced_anova_design(between = c(group = 2), within = c(time = 2)) m <- design_term_means(d, term = "group:time", target_pes = 0.2, n = 20) sim <- simulate_design_dataset(d, n = 20, means = m) head(sim) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   id    group   value time  #>   <fct> <fct>   <dbl> <fct> #> 1 1     group1  0.372 time1 #> 2 1     group1 -0.104 time2 #> 3 2     group1  0.911 time1 #> 4 2     group1 -0.440 time2 #> 5 3     group1  0.539 time1 #> 6 3     group1  1.08  time2

title: Summarise an anovapowersim power curve — summary.anovapowersim_curve
